message:
fix(web): make debug server stop() terminate promptly via nonblocking accept

The debug server's serve_loop used blocking listener.incoming() which
would not check the running flag until a new connection arrived, making
stop() hang indefinitely on idle servers.

Switch to a manual accept loop: set the listener to nonblocking mode,
loop while running.load(Relaxed), and on WouldBlock sleep 25ms before
retrying. This ensures stop() terminates the server within one poll
interval.

Update the WebSocket test to use read_to_end instead of a fixed buffer.
Add test stop_eventually_closes_listener validating the listener
actually closes after stop().

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
